item.title: About
item.description: Officialy, I’m ingineer in image and signal processing and PhD in robotics. In practice, I’m wondering what are the impacts of my actions on the society and if what i’m doing is usefull. I feel that our society is way to inequal and that most of those inequalities are baseless (for exemple, why is intellectual work more valued than manual work ?) and that’s why I try to put my skill into use in diverses software projects that aims at reducing the inequalities and that, for most of them, focus on associations and communities.

item.title: Contact
item.description: If you have a question on a specific project, the best way to get my attention is to open a github issue in the relevant repository. If you are not a github user or for anything else, feel free to contact me at : thibaut.tezenas(at)protonmail.com

item.title: Projects
item.description: [Just Starting] AIS4A : Automatic IT Setup for Associations Project schedule to begin in march 2020. Its goal is to allow anybody who wants to manage an association by using IT tools to install all the tools he will need in a few minutes. The main idee is to simplify the use of alternatives to Google/Slack/Trello/… to allow each association to own its data. [En BETA] Symphonium : A tool to help learning the piano Inspired by Synthesia (proprietary software) and PianoFromAbove (open source but deprecated), it is the only open source and cross platform software that helps you learn piano by vizualizing MIDIFiles and comparing thoses files to the notes you play on a MIDI keyboard.
